title: Medical Assistant Phlebotomy Laboratory
catalogDescription: Provides the skills and techniques for venipuncture and capillary blood collections, EKG, physical assessments and vital signs. Utilize veni-dot arms and peers to practice blood collections. Prepare and process samples for analysis. Learn to manage age specific needs, provide customer service, and obtain special collections. Meets the Medical Assistant Phlebotomy requirements to apply for credentialing through the State of Washington.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Concurrent enrollment in PHLEB 104 and HEALTH 127 ; and instructor consent.
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Concurrent enrollment in PHLEB 104 and NURSE 127 ; and instructor consent.

title: Medical Assistant Phlebotomy Laboratory
catalogDescription: This course is designed to equip students with the essential skills and knowledge required for a successful career in phlebotomy. Participants will engage in hands-on learning experiences to master the art of blood collection while also focusing on vital aspects of patient care and laboratory procedures. Will include how to manage age specific needs, provide customer service, and obtain special collections. Previously PHLEB 102.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Instructor permission.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Medical Assistant Phlebotomy
catalogDescription: This course provides students with a solid foundation in the theoretical aspects of phlebotomy, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of the principles, ethics, and regulations that govern this critical healthcare profession. Participants will delve into the core concepts that form the backbone of successful phlebotomy practice, setting the stage for a career characterized by precision, ethical conduct, and patient safety across the lifespan.

title: Advanced Phlebotomy Practicum
catalogDescription: Credits: 6 Consists of campus theory and lab as well as clinical experience in an assigned medical facility, to be arranged by the instructor. Provides practical experience in the role of the phlebotomy technician. Attendance is required to pass. Student must achieve a minimum grade of 2.5 or higher in order to pass the course. Meets the requirements for National Board Certification for a Phlebotomy Technician.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Completion of the Medical Assistant Phlebotomy Certificate at Green River College within 2 years of application, with a 2.5 GPA in all required courses. Liability Insurance and Health (injury and sickness) Insurance.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.
